latest.title: Canadian tin and critical metals exploration
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4764H
Rome Resources PLC
09 June 2026
9 June 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Update in relation to Canadian tin and critical metals exploration
Proposed 2026 field programme at New Brunswick Tin-Tungsten-Indium Project
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, is pleased to provide an update on the proposed 2026 field programme at the New Brunswick tin-tungsten-indium project in Eastern Canada.
As announced by the Company on 10 March 2026, the Company entered into an option agreement (the "Option") to acquire working interests in early-stage exploration licences across 109 km2 of mining claims in the Canadian Province of New Brunswick, close to the Mount Pleasant deposit, hosting the Three Lakes and Schoullar Mountain Projects (the "Projects"). It was further noted in the announcement that during the Option's period Rome Resources shall act as the operator of the licences connected to the Projects (the "Licences") and be responsible,
inter alia
, for carrying out exploration on the Licences.
It is expected that the 2026 field programme will commence this week. As operator, Rome Resources expects to incur a maximum of CA$150k in costs in relation to the 2026 field programme, following which it will assess its position regarding the exercise of the Option.
Highlights
·
2026 field programme commencing across three priority target areas in New Brunswick
·
Over 500 samples planned from surface and trench sampling
·
Land package subject to the Option includes areas adjacent to the historic Mount Pleasant Tungsten Mine, a past-producing tungsten operation
·
Field programme targets tin, tungsten and indium mineralisation in a highly prospective North American critical minerals district
Project overview
The Late Devonian Granite play of southwestern New Brunswick is a tin-tungsten-molybdenum-indium system with pervasive greisenisation in the later stages of intrusion. Substantial showings of the target minerals have been identified across the district.
While the district remains largely underexplored by modern standards, it includes the historic Mount Pleasant Tungsten Mine and numerous historical tin and tungsten workings.
The Company's decision to explore in New Brunswick reflects both the prospectivity of the district and its position within a stable North American mining jurisdiction. The region hosts extensive granite-related mineralisation, including critical minerals such as tin, tungsten and indium, commodities which continue to attract increasing strategic attention globally.
2026 field programme
The 2026 programme of fieldwork will focus on assessing the potential for exploration drilling across several tin and tungsten showings in three key areas of the Devonian granite play of southwestern New Brunswick (Figure 1).
The programme is expected to include over 500 samples which are likely to be taken for assays in a mixture of surface and trench sampling across the three main target areas.
Figure 1: Project areas subject to the Option
Key target areas
1. Schoullar Mountain
Located a few kilometres east of the historic Mount Pleasant Tungsten Mine and in the vicinity of an undeveloped tin zone, this target lies within the same broader mineralised system. The programme in this area will focus on sampling along four historical IP profiles, including trenching in two areas where the IP chargeability is interpreted to be close to surface. IP chargeability is a commonly used technique to detect sulphide and other metallic ore constituents.
2. Square Lake
Situated on the eastern greisenised margin of the Mount Douglas granite, this area will be sampled in and around the vicinity of several groups of historical workings for tin and tungsten.
3. Victoria Lake
(An expanded and re-named Three Lakes Project referred to in the Company's 10th March 2026 announcement).
This area comprises a large area of claims along the youngest greisenised phase of the Mount Douglas granite, where multiple showings of tin and tungsten have been identified. Grades of up to 1.4% tin have been identified in surface sampling.
The proposed work programme will combine surface sampling in the greisen along with targeted trenching in areas with near-surface tin mineralisation.
Provincial Support
In line with New Brunswick's stated policy of supporting mineral exploration, as operator, Rome Resources has been awarded an exploration grant covering a portion of its 2026 Field Programme expenditures.
The board of directors of Rome Resources (the "Board") are highly appreciative of this financial support, which underscores the Province's commitment to fostering mineral exploration.
Paul Barrett, Chief Executive Officer of Rome Resources, commented:
"The Mount Douglas granite system hosts one of the most prospective tin-tungsten-indium districts in eastern Canada, including the historic Mount Pleasant Tungsten Mine. The potential for tin, tungsten and indium resources in the Mount Douglas and associated granite systems potentially represents a significant opportunity for the Company and, with tungsten now firmly established as a strategically important critical mineral, we are delighted to be advancing the first field programme as operator across the project area.
The enthusiasm shown by the Province of New Brunswick for developing its resources demonstrates that we are pushing on an open door, and we look forward to potentially unlocking the potential of this highly prospective region, subject to the exercise of the Option.
The team continues to work towards an updated Mineral Resource Estimate for our Bisie North Project and will update the market on this along with the ongoing geophysical survey operations in due course."
For further information, please contact:

Dr. Armitage is a qualified person (QP) under NI 43-101 and as defined by the AIM Note for Mining, Oil and Gas Companies for the purposes of this announcement and has reviewed and approved the scientific and technical information contained in this news release.

Total Voting Rights
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, announces that the issued share capital of the Company as at the date of this announcement comprises 7,676,625,932 ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each with one voting right per share ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of Ordinary Shares and voting rights in the Company is 7,676,625,932.
The above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine if they are required to notify their interest in or a change to their interest in the share capital of the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the UK Financial Conduct Authority.

latest.title: Airborne Geophysical Survey
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0408E
Rome Resources PLC
13 May 2026
13
May 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Airborne Geophysical Survey
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, is pleased to announce that it is participating in an airborne geophysical survey (the "Survey") covering the greater Bisie tin district, a district that contains one of the world's highest grade tin plays.
The Survey forms part of a broader contiguous programme of geophysical data acquisition being conducted across adjacent areas of the wider Bisie tin district.
Acquisition of over 600km of helicopter-borne geophysical data is planned for Rome Resources, flown by Geotech Ltd, a globally renowned geophysical contractor, at 100m line spacing and comprising a full waveform time-domain electromagnetic sensor (VTEM Plus) coupled with a caesium magnetometer.
The Survey will cover the entirety of Rome Resources' two licences and is expected to help calibrate geophysical anomalies on Rome Resources' acreage with those of nearby producing mines. It is also expected to identify and prioritise additional tin exploration targets and better define the tin granite contact to the south in Rome Resources' licence areas.
Pre-Survey flight testing commenced in the southern area on 11 May 2026 and is expected to move onto Rome Resources' licences area in late May 2026.
Paul Barrett, Chief Executive Officer of Rome Resources, commented:
"The opportunity to undertake the Survey is very timely and represents an important step in advancing our understanding of the broader Bisie mineralisation system. Alongside the assay results from our recently completed drilling campaign at Kalayi and the forthcoming updated Mineral Resource Estimate, the Survey is expected to provide critical data to guide our plans for the next phase of drilling at both Kalayi and Mont Agoma, while also helping to assess the wider potential across our two licence areas as we define the next phase of exploration at Bisie North. We look forward to sharing the results in due course."
For further information, please contact:

latest.title: Update research note published by Allenby Capital
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8524D
Rome Resources PLC
12 May 2026
REACH announcement*
12 May 2026
Rome Resources PLC
("Rome Resources" or the "Company")
Update research note published by Allenby Capital
Rome Resources Plc (AIM: RMR), the Democratic Republic of Congo
‑
focused tin and base metals explorer, highlights
the publication of a new research note by
Allenby Capital, Rome Resources' joint broker and adviser, on 6 May 2026, titled "
Bullish Backdrop
".
The note reviews Rome Resources' recent operational and corporate progress at its Bisie North project including:
·
the latest drilling programme at Kalayi where wider
and higher-grade tin intercepts at depth continue to support the Company's evolving structural model;
·
Rome Resources' proposed increase in ownership interest across the Bisie North licences,
increasing its attributable exposure to future resource growth; and
·
the upcoming Mineral Resource Estimate update, alongside
further planned exploration activity including airborne geophysics and additional drilling.
The full research note is available on Allenby Capital's website at:
https://www.allenbycapital.com/client/rome-resources-plc/

latest.title: Subscription update and additional issue of equity
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3973D
Rome Resources PLC
07 May 2026
7 May 2026
Rome Resources plc
("
Rome Resources
" or the "
Company
")
Update regarding additional subscription
and
further issue of equity to settle investor relation fees
Rome Resources plc (AIM: RMR), the DRC-focused tin and copper explorer, provides the following update in respect of the Company's additional Subscription as announced on 27 April 2026 (the "
Additional Subscription
").
The Company previously announced that it expected 130,000,000 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM on or around 5 May 2026. To date, the Company has received commitments in relation to 50,000,000 new Ordinary Shares (approximately £150k) pursuant to the Additional Subscription. The Company expects the outstanding balance to be received in due course following a successful resolution of banking delays relating to the outstanding subscribers.
Additional issue of equity
Separately, the Company announces the issue of 9,600,000 new Ordinary Shares as payment in lieu of approximately £28,800 in accrued professional investor relation fees.
Admission and Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange plc for the 139,600,000 new Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on AIM. It is currently anticipated that Admission will become effective and that dealings in the new Ordinary Shares will commence on AIM at 8.00 a.m. on or around 12 May 2026.
Upon Admission, Rome Resources issued ordinary share capital will comprise 7,676,625,932 Ordinary Shares. Upon Admission, this number will represent the total voting rights in the Company, and, following Admission may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Unless otherwise defined herein, the capitalised defined terms used in this announcement have the same meaning as those used in the Company's announcement on 27 April 2026.
For further information, please contact:

company_30s.what_they_do: RMR Ltd. is a small‑cap exploration company focused on mining minerals such as tin and other base metals. They conduct geological surveys, airborne mapping and drilling to identify potential ore bodies.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tin is a key component in electronics and renewable‑energy equipment, and demand is rising as battery and solar technologies grow. RMR’s discoveries could supply a niche market that is currently under‑served, giving the company a competitive edge if the resource proves viable.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in the exploration phase, raising capital through subscriptions and issuing new shares. It has recently finished a drilling program and is awaiting the results of an airborne survey. The share price is trading well below its historic peaks, reflecting the steep fall that has attracted contrarian interest.

rns_narrative: The company has just wrapped up a multi‑week drilling program that produced the widest intercept to date, indicating a promising geological structure.
An airborne geophysical survey was launched in mid‑May, which should sharpen the target area and help estimate the size of the deposit.
In parallel, RMR is raising fresh capital through a subscription and a new equity issue, showing that investors are still willing to support the project.
Governance updates (director appointments, voting rights changes, and a general meeting outcome) suggest the board is keeping the company compliant and ready for future financing.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around fresh drilling data
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Edzi and MarginMiner highlight the latest drilling success and the potential for a commercial‑grade deposit, arguing that the company is finally moving beyond exploration.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Micksnags1 points out that the latest trade was a sell and that support is weak; he fears the company may struggle to turn the discovery into cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: After the March 24 drilling announcement, chatter shifted from cautious to mildly bullish, but remains tempered by the need for a confirmed resource estimate.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for RMR is flat – no public search activity. This silence means any news release could create a shock that the market is not yet priced for.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the airborne survey results (expected late May) – a significant positive finding could trigger a surge in share price.
Completion of a resource estimate or a letter of intent from a mining partner – this would provide a clear path to production.
A capital‑raising milestone (e.g., subscription close or new equity issue) that demonstrates investor confidence and reduces debt risk.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 3‑month average and a price break above the recent low. A confirmed resource estimate or a partnership letter will be the decisive confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates the price is still in a compressed, early‑stage of a rebound. The company has likely passed the most severe capitulation point; the next step is a clear catalyst that signals a move towards production.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share price rebounds to the midpoint between its all‑time low and the last high, the upside would be roughly 100% from the current level. The company’s recent drilling success and upcoming airborne data suggest a high probability of a resource confirmation, which historically has produced large bounces in similar deep‑crash scenarios.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the new data fails to translate into a commercial‑grade resource, leaving the company with high costs and no near‑term revenue. If the next announcement is negative or if the company cannot secure additional funding, the price could slide further towards the bottom.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is >3× the downside risk. If the stock can recover to 50% of its previous peak, the return is roughly 200% from today. The worst realistic downside is a further 10% drop before a full stop‑loss is triggered, giving a 1:3 risk‑reward ratio.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the recent low (around the 3‑month moving average) after a volume spike. Allocate 5% of your portfolio to the position. Place a stop 10% below the entry level to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the airborne survey results are announced or until a resource estimate is released. Watch for a 2‑to‑3× average daily volume and a price break above the recent high as confirmation.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure funding or announces a significant cost overrun, consider exiting to avoid further loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: RMR sits in a deep crash, fresh drilling data and an upcoming airborne survey could ignite a rebound, and the upside potential far outweighs the limited downside risk.
